title: NASA is making a powerful new ion engine to send astronauts to Mars — and it just passed its 1st test
summary: NASA has developed a new ion engine using lithium metal vapor as a propellant, achieving 25 times more power than the Psyche mission's engine in its first test. This advancement aims to support future astronaut missions to Mars.

title: Don't miss the Eta Aquarid meteor shower 2026 peak tonight! Viewing times, locations and tips
summary: The Eta Aquarid meteor shower is set to peak in the early hours of May 6, 2026, with details on viewing times, locations, and tips available.

title: NASA wants to land astronauts on the moon in 2028. Will SpaceX's Starship or Blue Origin's Blue Moon lander be ready in time?
summary: NASA aims to land astronauts on the moon by 2028, but the success hinges on two unproven commercial landers, SpaceX's Starship and Blue Origin's Blue Moon, which face significant technical and timeline challenges.

title: Trump's proposed NASA budget is a 'horrible threat to our future' in space, Planetary Society CEO says
summary: The Planetary Society CEO criticized Trump's proposed NASA budget, calling it a major threat to future space exploration. The organization is advocating to restore NASA's funding by 2027.

title: James Webb Space Telescope directly studies an exoplanet's surface for the 1st time: 'We see a dark, hot, barren rock'
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to directly study the surface of a distant super-Earth exoplanet for the first time, describing it as a dark, hot, barren rock similar to Mercury.

title: Watch the Eta Aquarid meteor shower 2026 online tonight with these livestreams
summary: The Eta Aquarid meteor shower in 2026 can be watched online through livestreams from observatories and sky cams worldwide.

title: Are streamers losing faith in legacy sci-fi franchises?
summary: The cancellation of 'Starfleet Academy' raises concerns about the future of 'Star Trek' and 'Star Wars' franchises. Streamers may be losing faith in legacy sci-fi content.

title: Our Milky Way's 'Zone of Avoidance' holds a galaxy supercluster with 30,000 trillion times the sun's mass
summary: Astronomers discovered the Vela Supercluster in the Milky Way's 'Zone of Avoidance,' with a mass 30,000 trillion times that of the Sun. This supercluster exerts significant gravitational influence on nearby galaxies.

title: Lasers take aim at a galaxy far, far away | Space photo of the day for May 4, 2026
summary: The ESO's Very Large Telescope Interferometer (VLTI) used four lasers to observe the Tarantula Nebula, creating a scene reminiscent of 'Star Wars'. This technology is part of advanced astronomical observations.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission. This period may be a calm before a busier launch schedule.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit aboard a Falcon 9 rocket. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program, demonstrating its growing role in deploying multiple small satellites in a single launch.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its Starship Flight 12 mission, featuring a revised trajectory. The upcoming launch follows regulatory and technical preparations to resume Starship operations.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday, marking a significant milestone for the country's space program. The debut flight demonstrates advancements in Russia's launch capabilities.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A doctor with experience in mass casualty events and overseas service has been selected as the next NASA astronaut for an upcoming ISS mission. The astronaut discussed their upcoming mission in a recent interview.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the successful Artemis II launch, which carried four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's LEO satellites this week from multiple locations. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is making a return, while Atlas V and Ariane 6 also have scheduled launches.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic compounds on Mars, providing fresh clues about ancient life on the Red Planet. This finding marks a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur Cosmodrome to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the recent CRS NG-24 mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over the past 50 years. This publication commemorates ESA's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A unique thermal test, dubbed 'the great parachute bake-out,' was conducted to ensure the reliability of parachutes under extreme conditions. This test is crucial for future space missions requiring precise landing systems.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA’s ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for a Mars mission, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on 8–9 June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris, with an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The exact location of this achievement was not specified in the provided text.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Bridenstine Takes Over as Quantum Space CEO
summary: Jim Bridenstine, former NASA administrator, has become CEO of Quantum Space, returning to his background in national security and defense. The move marks his transition from space policy to private sector leadership.

title: Anduril Names Its SBI Team For Golden Dome
summary: Anduril has named its team for the Space-Based Interceptor (SBI) component of the Golden Dome missile defense architecture. The company is one of 12 prime contractors involved in this initiative.

title: ESA Taps Edge Aerospace for Space Cloud Contract
summary: ESA has selected Edge Aerospace, a Luxembourg-based company, to develop an architecture and roadmap for orbital data centers. This contract aims to advance space cloud technology under a new initiative.

title: Lt. Gen. Schiess Nominated to Lead Space Force
summary: Lt. Gen. Schiess has been nominated to lead the U.S. Space Force, potentially becoming its third chief of space operations since the force's establishment in 2019. The nomination is pending confirmation.

title: Payload Field Guide: Lunar Rovers
summary: NASA and its partners are advancing lunar rover technology to support sustained human presence on the Moon, moving beyond Apollo-era designs to meet long-term operational needs.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: Space nuclear executives are optimistic about NASA's FY27 budget proposal, which Administrator Isaacman describes as the agency's 'logical evolution.' NASA is significantly supporting this initiative.

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: House appropriators have approved a $24.4 billion budget for NASA, rejecting the administration's FY27 request. This marks the first legislative step to formally oppose the administration's proposed spending plan.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space firms secured €1.4 billion in private capital in 2025, reflecting growing investor confidence in space industry startups.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has raised £9.8 million in seed funding to develop cancer drugs in orbit using microgravity, aiming to reformulate them for self-administration by patients.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has introduced a new satellite product designed for urban planning and monitoring. The tool aims to help construction firms, insurers, and city authorities assess risks and consequences of development projects.

title: NRO Makes New Commercial Awards to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) awarded new contracts for Earth sensing data to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel under its Strategic Commercial Enhancements (SCE) effort. This move expands the NRO's commercial satellite partnerships for enhanced data collection.

title: STMicroelectronics Expects its Space Business to Reach More than $3B Over Three Years
summary: STMicroelectronics, a semiconductor company supplying SpaceX and other space firms, expects its space business revenue to exceed $3 billion over the next three years.

title: Lockheed Martin Joins Seagate Space and Firefly Aerospace to Develop Sea-Based Launch
summary: Lockheed Martin has partnered with Seagate Space and Firefly Aerospace to develop a sea-based launch platform. The collaboration aims to enhance offshore launch capabilities.

title: Future-Proofing Satellite Networks With ST Engineering iDirect Sales SVP Brian Jakins
summary: ST Engineering iDirect's Sales SVP Brian Jakins discusses strategies to future-proof satellite networks amid advancements like 5G. The article highlights the importance of adapting satellite networks to thrive in an increasingly connected world.

title: Vantor Nabs $70 Million NGA Award For GEOINT Portal Use
summary: Vantor secured a $70 million contract from the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) for the first option year of a program involving a GEOINT portal. The award highlights Vantor's role in supporting geospatial intelligence initiatives.

title: Lynk, EarthDaily, True Anomaly Launch Satellites on Weekend SpaceX Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX launched 45 payloads, including satellites from Lynk, EarthDaily, and True Anomaly, on a rideshare mission for a South Korean Earth Observation satellite. This was part of a weekend mission to deploy multiple payloads into orbit.

title: Loft to Build 10 EO Satellites for French Constellation With Magellium Artal Group
summary: Loft will build 10 Earth Observation (EO) satellites for a French constellation in partnership with Magellium Artal Group. This collaboration marks a significant step in France's next-generation EO satellite initiatives.

title: Orbital Warfare Systems Top Priority for Space Chief Nominee Lt. Gen. Schiess
summary: The White House's nominee to succeed Gen. B. Chance Saltzman prioritizes orbital warfare systems. Lt. Gen. Schiess has emphasized developing more systems for orbital warfare.

title: Sateliot and Telefónica España to Explore 5G Satellite Connectivity for Defense, Military Uses
summary: Sateliot and Telefónica España have partnered to explore 5G satellite connectivity for defense, military, and remote area applications using Sateliot's satellite IoT technology.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discussed how Lite Coms designs its products to provide tactical, multi-orbit connectivity at Modern Day Marine. The conversation highlighted the company's focus on network resiliency.
